IN WEIGHING DEFENDANT'S RIGHT TO PRIVACY VERSUS PLAINTIFFS STATUTORY DISCOVERY RIGHTS THE COURT..... SEE MINUTE ORDER.

AMENDED LAW AND MOTION, MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENA DUCES TECUM, REVIEW OF DEFENDANT'S BANK RECORDS SET FOR JANUARY 30, 1998, 3 PM, RM 501. THE MATTER IS TO BE HELD IN CAMERA WITH DEFEN- DANT'S COUNSEL TO BE PRESENT; HOWEVER,IF DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL FAILS TO APPEAR THE RELEVANT RECORDS AS DETERMINED & ADJUDI- CATED BY THE COURT SHALL BE MAILED TO PLTF & DEFT COUNSEL.

REMITTITUR FILED SUPERIOR COURT FINDS THAT JUDGMENT IMPROPERLY AWARDED DAMA- GES FOR THE PERIOD OF 3-1-97 TO 3-10-97, IN THE AMOUNT OF $228.70. HERE, DAMAGES WERE CALCULATED @ $22.87 A DAY BEGINNING 3-1-97, YET THE NOTICE PERIOD DID NOT END UNTIL 3-10-97. AS SUCH, THE JUDGMENT SHOULD BE REDUCED BY $228.70 (10 DAYS X $22.87/DAY), FROM $11,145.56 TO $10,916.86.

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING AMENDED JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF RECOVER FROM DEFENDANT KING, ANN AKA DORIS ANN KING AKA DORIS KING AKA A. KING AKA D.
